title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. sphondylium
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. sphondylium, also known as wild parsnip, is a biennial plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of furanocoumarins, specifically bergapten and psoralen. The health benefits of Heracleum sphondylium subsp. sphondylium include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve wound healing. Wild parsnip can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
